name: Barbecued Chicken (S.davis)
recipeIngredient: 1 tbsp Chinese chili paste with garlic
recipeInstructions:
1. Precook the chicken in the microwave or gently poach it on the stovetop before finishing it on the grill to ensure that the meat is cooked through but the sauce is not charred. If you prefer a hotter sauce, increase the amount of chili paste.
2. Arrange chicken pieces in a micro-safe dish, with the thickest parts toward the outside of the dish. Cover with vented plastic wrap and microwave at HIGH (100%) for 8 to 10 minutes, or until tender, turning pieces twice.
3. Alternatively, gently simmer the chicken in cold water covered with a wide saucepan, skimming off any froth, until it is tender, about 10 to 12 minutes.
4. Meanwhile, prepare the grill or preheat the broiler.
5. In a small bowl, whisk together the remaining ingredients for the barbecue sauce.
6. With tongs, remove the chicken from the dish or saucepan and brush both sides generously with barbecue sauce.
7. Grill over medium-high heat or broil, basting often with the barbecue sauce until the chicken is glazed on the outside and no longer pink inside, about 5 minutes on each side.
